articles.title: 25 leopards relocated from Maharashtra to Gujarat's Vantara facility: Minister
articles.description: The population of leopards has risen four times in 10 years. The State, which had 101 tigers seven-eight years back, now has 444, Maharashtra’s Forest Minister Ganesh Naik said

Following Trump's comments, the price of Brent Crude dipped below $100 before rising to $103. Check petrol, diesel prices in your city on Tuesday, 24 March.

It's a ​system CEO Shen Shaojie, 39, describes as a cost-saving departure from the way autonomous drive systems have been built and trained.

articles.title: Colombia plane crash: Death toll at 66, dozens missing in Hercules C-130 accident; rescue ops under way
articles.description: The accident involved a Lockheed Martin-built Hercules C-130 transport plane, which crashed after flying just 1.5 km after takeoff from Puerto Leguizamo. The ammo and explosives being carried on board the military transport plane also detonated as a result of the crash, making rescue difficult.

The company is in talks with major banks but may struggle to match local rates, making price negotiations a key hurdle in entering one of the world’s largest digital payments markets.

articles.title: Govt to rejig PLI 2.0 sops on high GPU prices, AI server tech
articles.description: The terms of the production-linked incentive (PLI) scheme for IT hardware need to be re-evaluated due to the rising cost of graphics processing units (GPUs) and the emergence of artificial intelligence (AI) servers, a senior government official said on Monday.

articles.title: Indian GCC talent taking up global leadership positions at a faster clip
articles.description: Global capability centres (GCCs) in India are moving up the corporate ladder, with nearly 20% of such centres enjoying real strategic authority, up from just 5% a decade ago, according to data from ANSR and Deloitte India. The number of global leadership roles based in India has zoomed to over 6,500 from just 115 in 2015 and is poised to reach 30,000 by 2030 with AI expected to boost the trend, industry estimates suggest.

articles.title: AWS eyeing 2-3 gigawatts India capacity on tax sops sweetener
articles.description: AWS is targeting a long-term scale-up to 2-3 gigawatts of capacity after the government declared a 20-year tax holiday for cloud companies. India’s current data centre capacity is expected to increase to 10 gigawatts by 2030 from 1.4 gigawatts at present.

articles.title: Taking stock of India’s 10 semiconductor projects under ISM 1.0
articles.description: Kaynes Technology’s subsidiary Kaynes Semicon is set to launch its OSAT plant in Sanand, Gujarat on March 31, with senior leaders expected at the event. The Rs 3,307 crore project marks a key step in India’s semiconductor push, with 10 approved projects worth Rs 1.6 lakh crore moving towards execution.

articles.title: 2029 Lok Sabha poll plan: Over 800 seats, 33% women's quota

This move will also enable the immediate implementation of the Women Reservation Act. Seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes will also rise. Women will receive one-third reservation within these categories.

Atlanta Airport, the world's busiest, saw wait time stretching beyond three hours over the weekend.

articles.title: As conflicts intensify in Sudan and South Sudan, fleeing civilians are caught within closed borders

articles.title: Kerala polls: 1,202 candidates file nominations as deadline ends

articles.title: EC letter with BJP Kerala seal triggers row; poll panel calls it 'clerical error'
articles.description: A political storm brewed in Thiruvananthapuram after an Election Commission letter bore the BJP Kerala unit's seal. The CPI(M) and Congress sharply criticized the Election Commission, alleging bias. articles.description: The government's move is part of a broader push to expand India’s quality infrastructure across both mass-market apparel and technical textiles, in line with India’s focus on improving manufacturing competitiveness and product reliability.

articles.title: From the Opinions Editor: On menstrual leave, Supreme Court has named the problem. Now, legislature must find the answer
Iran warns of ‘irreversible damage’ to regional infrastructure after Trump threatens to attack Tehran’s power plants
Xiaomi Pad 8 review: With smooth screen and premium build, is this tablet finally a true laptop replacement?
Consumer Justice Report: Weak consumer rights could weaken India’s economic growth
articles.description: India’s growth story rests on a confident middle class and credible institutions, but a stark failure in consumer complaint redressal threatens both. Delayed justice, weak capacity and rising pendency could slow consumption and get in the way of our economic ambitions.

articles.title: PM Modi calls high-level meeting to review West Asia crisis & India’s energy security
Congress awards Davanagere South ticket to Shamnur clan, stirs Muslim leaders’ anger
articles.description: The Congress party has named Samarth Shamnur as its candidate for the Davanagere South bypoll. This decision comes after intense lobbying from Muslim leaders. Shamnur is the grandson of the late Lingayat leader Shamnur Shivashankarappa. The party leadership held discussions with community leaders. The bypoll is scheduled for April 9. In Bagalkot, Umesh Meti was chosen as the candidate.

articles.title: How agriPV can turn India’s farms into dual-purpose powerhouses
articles.description: India’s ambitious energy transition goals — to have 300 GW of installed solar capacity by 2030 and achieve net-zero emissions by 2070 — put land at a premium. Utility-scale solar projects require large tracts of land while agriculture is already under pressure from competing land use. Agri-photovoltaics can ameliorate this conflict

articles.title: Crude proposal: The US shouldn’t try to rig oil prices by intervening as a short seller
articles.description: Trying to manipulate the oil futures market may seem like a quick fix for high fuel prices, but it’ll warp price signals. Also, since real oil demand and supply must eventually come to bear, bad bets could lose the US treasury a lot of money.

articles.title: WTO talks: As power exertion intensifies, multilateralism has become more relevant, not less
articles.description: Global institutions are indispensable for emerging economies seeking a fairer deal as the rules-based order wobbles. With geopolitical heft setting tariff equations and the like, the World Trade Organization is what developing countries must turn to.

articles.title: Amit Shah lauds Modi's record 8,931 days in public life
articles.description: Home Minister Amit Shah lauded Prime Minister Narendra Modi for becoming the longest-serving head of government in India, surpassing 8,931 days in public life. Shah highlighted Modi's dedication to nation-first governance, integrity, and tireless service, attributing the achievement to his unwavering commitment and the trust of the people.

articles.title: DGP monitoring probe into rape case against self-styled godman, no one will be spared: Maharashtra  CM Fadnavis
articles.description: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis assured a high-level inquiry, monitored by the DGP, into the rape case involving self-styled godman Ashok Kharat. He stated no one will be spared and urged those with evidence to cooperate, while criticizing attempts to politicize the matter. Fadnavis also commented on political statements by Mamata Banerjee and Rahul Gandhi.

articles.title: Elon Musk launches 'Terafab' project to make own AI chips
articles.description: The "Terafab", a manufacturing facility based near Austin, Texas, will aim to produce one terawatt of computing power per year, says Elon Musk

articles.title: CEC Gyanesh Kumar acting under 'executive's thumb', says Opposition's notice seeking election chief's removal
articles.description: Opposition MPs have submitted notices in Parliament seeking the removal of Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar. Charges include acting under executive influence and causing mass disenfranchisement through the SIR exercise. The notices cite seven grounds of proved misbehaviour. Opposition leaders are awaiting government action on these notices.

articles.title: Manu Joseph: Why India’s potholes seem poised to become a political issue at last
articles.description: For decades, potholes, filth and bad roads were dismissed as trivial irritants, unworthy of political attention. That may be changing. As middle-class frustration builds and civic anger spills into public debate, the quality of life could actually matter at the ballot booth.

articles.title: 'Modi Ji, ab jhola uthao or chale jaiye': Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Sanjay Raut slams Centre over inflation
articles.description: "There will be a century...Abki baar, 100 ke paar. Where is Modi ji? He is in West Bengal, preparing for President's Rule to defeat Mamata Banerjee. Modi ji and Shah ji are attempting to do 'khela' in West Bengal, and the value of the rupee is declining...Modi ji was saying that when the value of the rupee falls, the country's prestige falls. What is it which is falling now?...," he said.

articles.title: ‘He gave up comfort… to serve’: Modi breaks 8,930-day record to become India's longest-serving head of Govt
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Sunday created history by becoming the longest-serving head of a government in India, surpassing former Sikkim Chief Minister Pawan Kumar Chamling.

articles.title: Don’t mourn Dubai yet. It can’t be destroyed by Iranian missiles
Iran Threatens to Hit Key Infrastructure After Trump’s Ultimatum
articles.description: Iran on Sunday warned it will attack key infrastructure across the Middle East if President Donald Trump follows through on his threat to “obliterate“ Tehran’s power plants unless the Strait of Hormuz swiftly reopens.

articles.title: ECI orders minimum facilities, voter assistance at all polling stations
articles.description: The Election Commission of India is preparing for upcoming Assembly polls in Assam, Kerala, Puducherry, Tamil Nadu, and West Bengal. All 2,18,807 polling stations will have essential facilities like drinking water and shade. Voters will receive assistance and information through posters and booths. A mobile phone deposit facility will also be available. These measures aim for a smooth voting experience.

articles.title: India should prepare for a world realigned after the West Asia war
articles.description: We must stay away from foreign wars—but not miss the opportunities emerging from a likely reset of global relations. As West Asia’s conflict rejigs alliances and exposes new fractures, India must combine strategic restraint abroad with clever outreach.
